spans.text: fertile by the labours of the swain
Britannia die : Yet shall this land with rising pomp divine , In it's own splendour and Britannia's shine
pomp divine , In it's own splendour and Britannia's shine
the bosom of this western land
you streams in hollow channels penned , Forever wasting , yet not ever spent
No flying scorpion darting instant death
No flying scorpion darting instant death
his bloody rounds , Nor bird of night her hateful note resounds
arose she over yonder eastern down
a larger load to bear Of fruits nectarine blooming once a year
The welcome thousands of the wheaten sheaves
for its master and the poor
With thankful heart to generous souls and kind , That save him from the winter and the wind
the ocean wave , Scarce heard a murmur but what ocean gave : Small it's circumference , nor high it's coast , But shady woods the happy isle could boast
every side new prospects caught the eye , There
No shouting armies their mad joy confessed , For battles gained , or trophies
No shouting armies their mad joy confessed , For battles gained , or trophies
No clamorous , crews , or wild nocturnal cheer , Or murderous russians for no men were
his prey , Transfixed the goat before the dawn of day
the goat before the dawn of day
The fruit of wanderings through each byway road
grateful they , The annual labours of his hands to pay
The annual labours of his hands
died the homely shepherd swain : Had seen his children and his children's heirs
the homely shepherd swain : Had seen his children and his children's heirs
his children and his children's heirs
To agriculture's first fair service bent

to change no country for his own : So had he with his fair endearing wife , Passed the slow circle of a harmless life ; With happy ignorance divinely blessed , The path , the centre and the home of rest
long the moon her mantled visage rear

spheres harmonious either sung or wept : He had not dreamt of worlds besides his own , And thought them only stars , beyond the moon ; Enjoyed himself , nor heard of future hell , Or heaven , the recompense of doing well ; Had scarcely thought of an eternal-state

either sung or wept : He had not dreamt of worlds besides his own , And thought them only stars , beyond the moon ; Enjoyed himself , nor heard of future hell , Or heaven , the recompense of doing well ; Had scarcely thought of an eternal-state
them only stars , beyond the moon
them only stars , beyond the moon
this isle such souls sublime contained
OH had this isle such souls sublime contained , And there for ages future sons remained , But envious time conspiring with the sea
Yet this wide land it's place can well supply With landscapes , hills and grassy mountains high
Yet this wide land it's place
Yet this wide land it's place
hills over hills , a never ending view
the mistress of the conquered earth
strong JUDEA'S once thrice holy land
strong JUDEA'S once thrice holy land
The earth itself to brighter days aspire , And wish to feel the purifying fire
The plundering wretch , or man of bloody mind : Renowned SACHEMS once their empires raised On wholesome laws ; and sacrifices blazed
every tribe resolved to be our foes
Then bloody wars , and death and rage
Then bloody wars , and death and rage
beneath another sky , PORT NELSON , and each BRITISH factory
In slender boats from distant lands they sail , Their small masts bending to the inland gale , On traffic
the little store , Which keeps them plenteous
Each for their fate most manfully prepared , From bubbling waves to send the parting breath To lands of shadows
COLMA as they'd mourn a friend
their COLMA as they'd mourn a friend
while in waves the bark demerged
the hapless wife , OH save our son , and save thy dearer life : It's thou canst teach him how to hunt the do , Transfix the buck , or tread the mountain snow
firm as death , Resolved to save her with his latest breath
save her with his latest breath
the infant from the chilling tide
thee youthful in thy gayest pride , With captives , trophies , victors at thy side
a nightless sky , Far to the west , where radiant Sol descends , And wonders where the arched horizon
His dauntless mind , nor death nor danger
to my tomb through yonder furzy glade , And show where thou thy much loved COLMA laid
where thou thy much loved COLMA laid
I see thee in each ghostly shoal , And think I clasp thee to my weary soul
I clasp thee to my weary soul
an angel , whom I loved a man
sad CAFFRARO all his grief declares

senseless stupid to the shore is born In deathlike slumbers , till the rising morn , Then sorrowing , to the sea his course he bent Full sad , but knew not for what cause he went , Till , sight distressing , from the lonely strand , He saw dead COLMA wafting to the land
the torrent of the gulf with tears
to the shore is born In deathlike slumbers
to the shore is born In deathlike slumbers
what cause he went , Till , sight distressing , from the lonely strand , He saw dead COLMA wafting to the land
dead COLMA wafting to the land
in a stupid agony of prayer
wished a place among the dead
whispering angels come to his relief
Then had the floods their peaceful courses kept , Nor the sad pine in all it's murmurs wept ; Nor pensive deer strayed through the lonely grove , Nor sadly wept the sympathising dove
the rocks not echoed to his pain
the floods their peaceful courses kept
now perhaps in some strange fancied land
Where now perhaps in some strange fancied land

Whether the firy kingdom of the sun , Or the slow wave of silent Acheron , Or Christian's heaven , or planetary sphere , Or the third region of the cloudless air ; Or if returned to dread nihility , You'll still be happy , for you will not be
the third region of the cloudless air
by the labours of the swain
Which scarce the hoary wrinkled sage
The tempting beauties of this spacious ball

fin my rigid breast did stain , Thou poetry shouldst be the darling sin ; Which heaven without repentance might forgive , And which an angel might commit and live : And where yonder wave of silent water falls , Over the smooth rock or Adamantine walls : The summer morns and vernal eves should see , MILTON , immortal bard my company ; Or SHAKESPEARE , DRYDEN , each high sounding name , The pride of BRITAIN , and one half her fame : Or him who waked the fairy muse of old , And pleasing tales of lands enchanted told
